description: Comms station manages: IFF transponder (broadcast identity ‚Äî can be spoofed or turned off), radio communications (speed-of-light delay at long range), contact hailing, and distress signals. IFF is not magic identification ‚Äî it's a broadcast that can be faked. Without IFF, contacts must be identified by sensor analysis (drive signature, radar cross-section profile, visual identification at close range). Comms commands: hail-contact, set-transponder, broadcast-message, set-emcon-level (emissions control ‚Äî reduce or eliminate all active emissions for stealth at cost of sensor capability).
title: Communications & IFF System

Without IFF, contacts must be identified by sensor analysis (drive signature, radar cross-section profile, visual identification at close range). summary:
## Summary: Comms & IFF System

### Changes Implemented
- **CommsSystem** (`hybrid/systems/comms_system.py`): New ship system inheriting from BaseSystem that manages:
  - **IFF transponder**: Enable/disable/spoof identity broadcast. IFF is a cooperative broadcast that can be faked or turned off ‚Äî without it, contacts must be identified by sensor analysis.
  - **Radio communications**: Speed-of-light delay calculation (`distance / c`), directed hailing with propagation delay display, broadcast messaging on named channels (GUARD default).
  - **Distress beacon**: Continuous emergency signal, toggleable on/off.
  - **EMCON integration**: When ECM system's EMCON mode is active, transponder and distress beacon are silenced automatically (user's desired setting preserved, just suppressed).
  - **Message log**: Retains last 50 messages (hails, broadcasts, distress events).

- **Command handlers** (`hybrid/commands/comms_commands.py`): 5 commands registered:
  - `set_transponder` ‚Äî enable/disable transponder, set/spoof IFF code
  - `hail_contact` ‚Äî hail specific contact with optional message (shows signal delay)
  - `broadcast_message` ‚Äî broadcast on a radio channel
  - `set_distress` ‚Äî activate/deactivate distress beacon
  - `comms_status` ‚Äî full system readout

- **3-point command registration**:
  - `hybrid/command_handler.py` ‚Äî 5 entries mapping commands to `("comms", action)`
  - `hybrid/commands/dispatch.py` ‚Äî imports and calls `comms_commands.register_commands()`
  - `server/stations/station_types.py` ‚Äî COMMS station gets all 5 commands plus `set_emcon` and `ecm_status`

- **System registry** (`hybrid/systems/__init__.py`): Added `CommsSystem` to `system_map`, imports, and `__all__`

- **Telemetry** (`hybrid/telemetry.py`): Added `_get_comms_state()` helper and `comms` key in `get_ship_telemetry()` output

- **GUI comms-control-panel** (`gui/components/comms-control-panel.js`): Web Component with Shadow DOM featuring:
  - Transponder section (status display, enable/disable toggle, IFF code input)
  - Radio section (hail contact input, broadcast message + channel selector)
  - Distress beacon section (red pulsing toggle button)
  - Message log section (scrollable, color-coded by message type)
  - Top-level mode indicator (ACTIVE/SILENT/EMCON/DISTRESS/OFFLINE)

- **View integration**:
  - `gui/components/view-tabs.js` ‚Äî Added COMMS tab at position 5 (keyboard shortcut 5), renumbered Science‚Üí6, Fleet‚Üí7, Mission‚Üí8, Editor‚Üí9
  - `gui/index.html` ‚Äî Added `#view-comms` section with comms panel, sensor contacts, tactical map, and event log; registered in views map; updated station-view mapping
  - `gui/js/main.js` ‚Äî Added import for `comms-control-panel.js`
  - `gui/components/status-bar.js` ‚Äî Added `_getCommsHtml()` showing COM: IFF code, SILENT, EMCON, or DISTRESS states

### Files Modified
- `hybrid/systems/comms_system.py` (new ‚Äî 298 lines)
- `hybrid/commands/comms_commands.py` (new ‚Äî 158 lines)
- `gui/components/comms-control-panel.js` (new ‚Äî ~765 lines)
- `hybrid/command_handler.py` (added 5 comms command mappings)
- `hybrid/systems/__init__.py` (registered CommsSystem)
- `hybrid/commands/dispatch.py` (import + register comms_commands)
- `server/stations/station_types.py` (populated COMMS station commands)
- `hybrid/telemetry.py` (added _get_comms_state + comms in telemetry output)
- `gui/components/view-tabs.js` (added COMMS tab, renumbered shortcuts)
- `gui/index.html` (added Comms view section and view registration)
- `gui/js/main.js` (added comms-control-panel import)
- `gui/components/status-bar.js` (added COM status indicator)

### Verification Status
- Playwright tests (5/5 passed): Verified Comms tab appears in view-tabs, Comms view container exists, clicking Comms tab activates the view, comms-control-panel component renders, and keyboard shortcut 5 activates Comms view. Test file deleted after verification.

### Notes for Developer
- IFF is intentionally NOT magic identification ‚Äî it's a broadcast that can be spoofed via `set_transponder {code: "FRIENDLY_FACTION"}`. Without IFF, contacts appear as unknown and must be identified by sensor analysis.
- EMCON integration is automatic: when ECM system's EMCON mode is active, the transponder and distress beacon are silenced without changing the user's settings.
- Speed-of-light delay is computed for hails using actual ship positions ‚Äî at 500km range the delay is ~1.7ms (negligible), but at longer ranges it becomes significant for gameplay.
- The comms system is NOT in essential_systems ‚Äî ships only get it if `systems.comms` is in their config. To make it always available, add `"comms": {}` to the essential_systems dict in `ship.py`.
